item.title: approve-action tool saveQueue() writes approval queue without error handling
item.intent: Approval queue writes never fail silently. A disk write error in saveQueue() must be caught and logged — a silently dropped write means an approval is lost, directly violating the no-auto-send guarantee.
item.description: src/design/tools/approve-action.ts:59 defines a saveQueue() function that calls writeFileSync(QUEUE_FILE, JSON.stringify(queue, null, 2)) without try-catch. This is an agent-side script installed into the deployed agent's tools directory. If the file write fails (disk full, permission error), the approval is silently dropped — the user receives a Telegram approval notification but their approve/reject action is never persisted. The no-auto-send guarantee depends on this write succeeding. Note: this is a GENERATED file. The fix must be applied in the template/generator that produces it, not in the output file directly. Steps: (1) locate the generator for approve-action.ts in src/design/tools/ (likely tools.ts or approve-action generator); (2) update the saveQueue() template to wrap writeFileSync in try-catch with console.error(err) and process.exit(1) on failure (the tool is a CLI script — failure must be loud); (3) verify the fix propagates to newly generated tools by running design/tools/ tests; (4) if existing deployed approve-action.ts files are not regenerated automatically, note in the fix that re-running 'clawhq init' or 'clawhq build' regenerates the tool. Scope: 1 generator file, ~5 lines added to template.
